title: osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda
intro: Osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda, commonly known as the Hawaii roseapple or Hawaii rose, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands. It is a shrub or small tree with fragrant, white or pink flowers and edible fruits, which are rich in nutrients and antioxidants. The health benefits of Osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which exhibit therapeutic actions such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like hives, itching, and swelling. In rare cases, excessive consumption may lead to more severe complications, including kidney damage and liver toxicity. Additionally, individuals with pre-existing medical conditions, such as diabetes or bleeding disorders, may experience worsened symptoms or interactions with medications. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using osteomeles anthyllidifolia var. subrotunda or any other herbal remedy.
